name: telegram
description: Two-way bridge between NTC and a Telegram bot — control sessions, receive idle/exit notifications, send commands when you're away from the app.

configSchema.label: Bot Token
configSchema.description: Token from @BotFather. Send /newbot in Telegram, follow the prompts, copy the token here. Or leave blank and set the NTC_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var on the server.

configSchema.label: Allowed Chat IDs
configSchema.description: Comma-separated Telegram chat / user IDs allowed to talk to the bot. Anything from a chat NOT in this list is silently dropped. Use @userinfobot to find your own ID.

configSchema.label: Notifications Chat ID
configSchema.description: Default chat that receives idle / exit / task-finish notifications. Defaults to the first allowed chat ID.

configSchema.label: Notify on Idle
configSchema.description: Send a Telegram message when a session goes idle (CLI waiting for input).

configSchema.label: Notify on Exit
configSchema.description: Send a Telegram message when a session exits.

configSchema.label: Notification Tail Lines
configSchema.description: How many lines from the terminal buffer to include in idle / exit notification messages.

configSchema.label: Poll Interval (seconds)
configSchema.description: Telegram getUpdates long-poll timeout in seconds. The bot holds a connection open for this duration — if a message arrives during this window it is delivered instantly (not delayed). 25 is the recommended value. Values below 5 increase API call frequency without improving responsiveness.

configSchema.label: Extra Claude Config Dirs
configSchema.description: Optional. Comma-separated list of Claude config roots (each the dir that contains 'projects/') to search for structured JSONL sessions, in addition to $HOME/.claude and any registered claude_accounts. Usually leave blank — the bridge auto-discovers all enabled accounts. Only needed for non-standard CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR layouts.
